_README: Operator policy template for the Radar 'Policy' tab. This is a SAMPLE — the
live policy is stored in Postgres (governance.operator_policy); paste an edited
copy of this into the Policy tab and click 'Save policy'.
The 'agents' and 'tools' sections work IDENTICALLY — both use the model below.
AGENTS are enforced at the write-action gate (a denied agent is blocked) and
shown on the Policy tab's 'Agent recognition' table + the Radar 'recognised'
badge. TOOLS are enforced + shown on 'Tool Risk Tiers'.
DECISION MODEL (agents AND tools):
1. DEFAULT CHECK = the slider ('allowed risk level' on Tool Risk Tiers). An
agent/tool is ALLOWED when its risk tier is BELOW the slider, DENIED at or
above it. For an AGENT the tier is its authoritative risk; for a TOOL it is
the SLM-reasoned tier.
2. EXPLICIT OVERRIDE = the only thing that beats the slider:
'allow': true  -> always allowed (whitelist, even above the slider).
'allow': false -> always denied  (even below the slider).
3. 'risk' RELABELS the tier the slider compares against.
Omitting a field — or setting it to the string "default" — means 'no opinion,
use the slider / OPA-suggested value'. allow:"default" and risk:"default" behave
exactly as if the field were absent.
Keys may be an exact agent id/name or a glob ('agent-crewai-*', '*temporal*');
tool keys are the tool name (globs allowed). Agent risk -> low|medium|high;
tool risk -> low|medium|high|severe. Keys beginning with '_' are ignored.
NOTE: the slider-default for agents needs the OPA agent running (it owns the
slider); without it, only explicit agent allow/deny apply.
note: example policy — demonstrates every agent + tool capability

*temporal*.note: EXPLICIT DENY  — infra process, never recognised

Python.note: EXPLICIT DENY  — bare interpreter, not a task agent

agent-crewai-*.note: EXPLICIT ALLOW — whitelist past the slider, even at high risk

agent-langgraph-*.note: RISK OVERRIDE  — relabel to low so the slider lets it through

agent-pydantic_ai-*.note: DEFAULT       — defer to the slider (same as omitting allow)

run_command.note: EXPLICIT DENY  — shell exec, always blocked

external_post.note: EXPLICIT DENY  — outbound write, always blocked

web_search.note: EXPLICIT ALLOW — whitelist past the slider

save_artifact.note: RISK OVERRIDE  — relabel tier the slider compares against

search_arxiv.note: DEFAULT       — let the SLM tier + slider decide
